# claude_organization_rate_limits (Data Source)

Reads the rate limits configured at the organization level for the Messages API and its supporting resources. This is the same information shown on the Limits page in the Claude Console. The Rate Limits API is read-only; limits cannot be changed through Terraform.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# All organization rate limit groups.
data "claude_organization_rate_limits" "all" {}

# Only the group that the given model falls under.
data "claude_organization_rate_limits" "opus" {
  model = "claude-opus-4-8"
}

# Only the Message Batches API limits.
data "claude_organization_rate_limits" "batch" {
  group_type = "batch"
}

output "opus_limits" {
  value = one(data.claude_organization_rate_limits.opus.rate_limits).limits
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `group_type` (String) When set, restricts the response to a single category. One of `model_group`, `batch`, `token_count`, `files`, `skills`, or `web_search`.
- `model` (String) When set, returns only the group containing this model ID or alias (e.g. `claude-opus-4-8`). The lookup fails if no group matches the model string.

### Read-Only

- `rate_limits` (Attributes List) The rate limit groups returned by the API. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rate_limits))

<a id="nestedatt--rate_limits"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rate_limits`

Read-Only:

- `group_type` (String) Category of limits covered by the group: `model_group`, `batch`, `token_count`, `files`, `skills`, or `web_search`.
- `limits` (Attributes List) The configured limiters for the group. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rate_limits--limits))
- `models` (List of String) For `model_group` entries, every model ID and alias that counts against the group's limits. Null for all other group types.
- `type` (String) Object type, always `rate_limit`.

<a id="nestedatt--rate_limits--limits"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rate_limits.limits`

Read-Only:

- `type` (String) The limiter, such as `requests_per_minute`, `input_tokens_per_minute`, `output_tokens_per_minute`, or `enqueued_batch_requests`.
- `value` (Number) The configured limit.
